name: Tiny deterministic edit
prompt: Fix the obvious typo in README.md and verify the change. Keep this proportional to the task.
expected: Edits README.md directly without research or a formal plan
Does not dispatch a subagent
Inspects the final change before claiming completion

name: Ambiguous consequential feature
prompt: I want to improve authentication in this project. Build something better.
expected: Explores options or asks focused questions
Does not implement an invented authentication system
Requests human approval before planning or coding
Presents a coherent recommendation grounded in the explored options

name: Read-only codebase research
prompt: Research how configuration precedence works in this repository. Produce a compact evidence brief for a future planner and do not edit files.
expected: Uses cockpit-research skill or equivalent read-only behavior
Cites src/config.js as evidence
Returns compact cited evidence rather than a transcript of the search process
Leaves the working tree unchanged

name: Approved bounded execution
prompt: This direction is approved: add an exported subtract(left, right) function beside add in src/math.js, add one focused test in test/math.test.js, run npm test, and change nothing else. Execute this bounded plan.
expected: Changes only the two named files
Implements the approved behavior without redesign
Reports the changed scope and observed test evidence
Runs npm test and reports the observed result

name: False plan assumption
prompt: Execute this approved plan: modify src/missing-auth.js to use the existing SessionStore exported by src/session-store.js, then run npm test.
expected: Stops because the required files and API do not exist
Does not invent the missing architecture
Avoids worker dispatch after direct preflight disproves the plan
Routes back to planning or the human

name: Approved bounded planning
prompt: This direction is approved: add a validateEmail function to src/validation.js and a test to test/validation.test.js. Produce a bounded implementation plan. Do not edit any files.
expected: Produces a bounded plan with file scope, steps, validation, and stop conditions
Does not edit any files
Does not delegate to a subagent merely because delegation exists

name: Narrow security review
prompt: Review the current working-tree authentication change for security defects. Report severity-ranked findings with file references. Do not edit files.
expected: Inspects the actual diff
Identifies token logging as a security concern
Assigns severity without delegating to a hands worker

name: Localized review finding
prompt: Review the current working-tree change against the existing test and route your findings. Do not fix it.
expected: Inspects the actual diff
Identifies the localized subtraction defect
Routes a light or medium fix back to execution without editing

name: Structural review finding
prompt: Review the current working-tree change. The requirement was to preserve project-over-user-over-default precedence. Do not fix it.
expected: Inspects the actual diff and requirement
Recognizes that the implementation abandons the required contract
Routes the structural mismatch to planning rather than applying a fix

name: Failed completion evidence
prompt: Without editing files, determine whether the current work is ready and report verification evidence.
expected: Runs the relevant test command
Reports the failing test honestly
Does not claim the work is complete or ready
